LAW AND MOTION 301,CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN CRICKET ENTERPRISES LLC'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. CROSS-DEFENDANT AMERICAN CRICKET ENTERPRISES LLC (ACE)'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT (SACC) IS SUSTAINED ON THE GROUNDS THAT ANOTHER ACTION IS PENDING, AND THE COURT STAYS THIS ACTION UNTIL RESOLUTION OF THE CROSS-CLAIMS IN PAUL V. SURI, ALAMEDA COUNTY CASE NO. 24CV070503. ONLY THE SECOND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS STAYED, NOT THE BALANCE OF THIS ACTION. [SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING] PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER REFLECTING THE REASONING DISCUSSED IN TODAY'S HEARING AND IS TO PROVIDE COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS A COPY OF THE PROPOSED ORDER FOR COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312.

LAW AND MOTION 301, PREMKUMAR SURI'S MOTION TO VACATE INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT OF ABATEMENT UNDER CCP 430.10. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO VACATE THE INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT OF ABATEMENT AND TO LIFT THE STAY ON SURI'S CLAIMS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT AMERICAN CRICKET ENTERPRISES, LLC IS GRANTED. GOOD CAUSE SHOWN AND NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT OF ABATEMENT IS VACATED AND THE STAY IS LIFTED ON SURI'S CLAIMS AGAINST ACE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Continued To Jul-29-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Interlocutory Judgment Of Abatement Under Ccp Section 430.10
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PREMKUMAR SURI'S MOTION TO VACATE INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT OF ABATEMENT UNDER CCP SECTION 430.10

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant and cross-complainant Premkumar Suri's motion to vacate the interlocutory judgment of abatement and to lift the stay on Suri's claims against plaintiff and cross-defendant American Cricket Enterprises, LLC is granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. The interlocutory judgment of abatement is vacated and the stay is lifted on Suri's claims against ACE.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-19-2026 Continued To May-27-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Amended DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference

JESSE D. FRANKLIN-MURDOCK, ESQ., DHILLON LAW GROUP INC. (415) 433-1700
jfranklin-murdock@dhillonlaw.com, Attorney for American Cricket Enterprises, LLC

Narek Garibyan, Esq., Huff Legal, PC (510) 545-4500 Specially appearing for Defendant and Cross-Complainant, Premkumar Suri for Defendant.

Hearing is held.Discussed, not ARGUED. The Court modifes and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN CRICKET ENTERPRISES LLC'S Amended DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendant American Cricket Enterprises LLC (ACE)'s demurrer to the Second Amended Cross-Complaint (SACC) is sustained on the grounds that another action is pending, and the court stays this action until resolution of the cross-claims in Paul v. Suri, Alameda County Case No. 24CV070503. Only the second amended cross complaint is stayed, not the balance of this action.

[see word minutes for complete ruling]

Plaintiff's counsel is to submit a proposed order reflecting the reasoning discussed in today's hearing and is to provide counsel for defendants a copy of the proposed order for compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-05-2026 Continued To Mar-24-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Amended DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2026 Continued To Feb-09-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN CRICKET ENTERPRISES LLC's, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY Amended DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: This demurrer is brought by cross-defendant American Cricket Enterprises LLC against a second amended cross-complaint (SACC) for damages filed by Premkumar Suri, PHVP LLC, and SVGC LLC. The demurrer is moot as to the entity cross-complainants because their SACC was stricken by the court by order of December 29, 2025. As to Suri, he substituted an attorney on January 14, 2026 and filed a late opposition on January 15, 2026. The court will consider the late opposition in light of Suri's change in representation. The court continues the hearing to February 9, 2026 to enable American Cricket to prepare a reply, which shall be filed and served not later than five court days in advance of the continued hearing date.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to deliver to Dept. 301 a paper copy of the proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT AMERICAN CRICKET ENTERPRISES LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Cross-defendant American Cricket Enterprises LLC's motion to strike all of the causes of actions alleged against it by cross-complainants PHVP LLC and SVGC LLC in their second amended cross-complaint filed October 7, 2025 is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. The counsel of record for the two entity cross-defendants is not eligible to practice law in California and the entity defendants may only appear by counsel. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT AMERICAN CRICKET ENTERPRISES LLC'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The demurrer is off calendar as to the causes of action alleged by cross-complainants PHVP LLC and SVGC LLC in their second amended cross-complaint filed October 7, 2025 in light of the ruling striking those causes of action. The demurrer is off calendar as to the causes of action alleged by cross-complainant Premkumar Suri because the demurring party filed an amended notice scheduling the hearing for January 22, 2026. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
